occurs_when: An iRule is loaded or a virtual server is enabled; executes once per TMM instance, not per connection.
notes: Use to initialize tables, static variables.

occurs_when: A new flow (clientside) is recognized prior to L4 acceptance.
notes: Present on modern TMOS; occurs before CLIENT_ACCEPTED.

occurs_when: TCP three-way handshake completes and a clientside connection is accepted on the virtual server.
notes: Good place to select SNAT, set ip::tos, TCP::collect, or enable HTTP/SSL.

occurs_when: Clientsides closes before HTTP request (RST/FIN/timeout).
notes: May fire multiple times across keepalives; appears in red path of flowchart.

occurs_when: BIG-IP receives ClientHello on clientside TLS.
notes: Use to select certificates/SNI, renegotiate ciphers.

occurs_when: Client certificate is presented during TLS handshake (if requested/required).
notes: Only when client-auth is configured.

occurs_when: Result is available for an async AUTH::start or Access policy check.
notes: Green box in chart; only if iRule/auth is used.

occurs_when: Clientside TLS handshake completes successfully.
notes: HTTP traffic becomes visible to iRules after this, if HTTP profile is present.

occurs_when: Decrypted clientside TLS app-data arrives.
notes: Visible if HTTP is not enabled or when using raw TLS data logic.

occurs_when: A complete set of HTTP request headers is parsed.
notes: Most common event; fires on every HTTP request in keepalive.

occurs_when: HTTP request body chunk(s) arrive after headers when collect/streaming is enabled or content-length present.
notes: Use HTTP::collect to trigger.

occurs_when: Web Acceleration/HTTP cache is enabled and a cacheable request is evaluated.
notes: Legacy/optional; depends on profiles.

occurs_when: Deprecated HTTP Class profile selects a class for the request.
notes: HTTP Class is deprecated; kept for completeness from the chart.

occurs_when: Deprecated HTTP Class selection failed and control falls through.
notes: From older TMOS branches.

occurs_when: A pool member destination is selected for the request/connection.
notes: Fires once per request with OneConnect; otherwise per-connection.

occurs_when: Selected persist record is down/invalid and fallback is taken.
notes: Green note in chart near LB_FAILED path.

occurs_when: Load balancing/selection fails (no members/up, or filters deny).
notes: You can retry, reset, or select alternate pool.

occurs_when: A serverside TCP connection to the selected pool member is established.
notes: Good place to set TCP options serverside.

occurs_when: BIG-IP (as client to pool) sends ClientHello for serverside TLS (ServerSSL profile).
notes: Name appears as SERVERSSL_CLIENTHELLO in modern docs.

occurs_when: Serverside TLS handshake completes.
notes: After this, serverside HTTP becomes visible if parsing there.

occurs_when: Decrypted serverside TLS app-data arrives.
notes: Used if not in HTTP proxy mode.

occurs_when: Request headers are forwarded to the pool member.
notes: Useful for last-moment header edits before egress.

occurs_when: Serverside plaintext data is received prior to HTTP parsing (or without HTTP).
notes: With HTTP profile, you usually work in HTTP_RESPONSE instead.

occurs_when: A complete set of HTTP response headers is parsed from the server.
notes: Fires for each response in keepalive and for interim 1xx as supported.

occurs_when: Response is cacheable and cache is updated (Web Acceleration/HTTP Cache).
notes: Optional; depends on profiles.

occurs_when: Stream profile is enabled and a rule match occurs.
notes: Appears both on request and response sides in the chart.

occurs_when: HTTP response body chunk(s) arrive after headers when collect/streaming is enabled.
notes: Triggered by HTTP::collect or large bodies.

occurs_when: Issued when an iRule paused/collected body and then allows pipeline to continue.
notes: Use after processing collected chunks.

occurs_when: Final chance to modify/allow a buffered response right before it is released to the client.
notes: Occurs after full body assembly when buffering.

occurs_when: Serverside TCP connection closes (FIN/RST/timeout).
notes: Red path shows close can occur at many points.

occurs_when: Clientside TCP connection closes (FIN/RST/timeout) after response.
notes: End of transaction/connection.
